workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: I have this escalation email from Alpine Ski House that needs identify the blockers.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 13:24 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House renewal risk

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 11:58 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 10:08 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-J trap (Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-J failure mode (Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-J -- Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread
